rationale_for_baseline_tier: Acute uncomplicated cystitis in a non-pregnant woman with typical symptoms and no upper-tract/systemic features is appropriately managed with empirical short-course antibiotics and fluids via telehealth, with routine follow-up if not resolving (T1). Pyelonephritis/systemic infection routes to ED (T4); pregnancy/complicating factors route to GP for tailored management.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Fever or shakes/rigors, pain in the back or side (loin), nausea/vomiting, or feeling very unwell
escalation_edges.rationale: Pyelonephritis/systemic infection needing different treatment ± admission.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you develop a fever or shakes, pain in your back or side, nausea/vomiting, or feel very unwell — see a doctor / go to ED the same day.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: You could be pregnant, symptoms don't improve within 48 hours, this keeps recurring, or you develop vaginal discharge/irritation
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: GP review (pregnancy test / culture / tailored treatment)
escalation_edges.rationale: Pregnancy, treatment failure, recurrence or an alternative diagnosis needs GP assessment.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Give clear pyelonephritis red flags and a follow-up plan if not improving.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: A short course of antibiotics should clear this — take it as prescribed, drink plenty of fluids, and the burning should ease within a day. Wipe front-to-back and empty your bladder after sex to help prevent it recurring.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Empirical antibiotics + telehealth/GP follow-up; ED for pyelonephritis features; GP if pregnant/recurrent/not improving
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: GP/ED for fever, loin pain, vomiting or feeling very unwell.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Uncomplicated cystitis in a (presumed) non-pregnant woman; pyelonephritis and complicating factors screened negative; empirical nitrofurantoin/trimethoprim + fluids; confirm pregnancy status; culture if failed/recurrent.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Empirical antibiotics with no pyelonephritis screen/safety-net or no pregnancy check.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A pyelonephritis is treated as simple cystitis and progresses to urosepsis, or an unsafe antibiotic is given in pregnancy.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Not confirming pregnancy status / complicating factors.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED for uncomplicated cystitis with a negative upper-tract screen — unnecessary (telehealth empirical treatment is appropriate).
